item.title: Millions of Americans have until July 10 to claim refunds from the IRS
item.description: A federal court ruling enabled some taxpayers to seek refunds tied to COVID-era filing deadlines. But that window expires July 10 — here's what to know.

item.title: Man partially sucked out of plane window during flight, passenger says
item.description: A woman says she saw a fellow passenger on her Ryanair flight get his head and shoulders sucked out of a window that broke during their trip between Greece and Germany.

item.title: Trump again says Iran ceasefire is over, but U.S. will keep talking
item.description: Efforts are underway to get U.S.-Iran peace talks back on track after the most intense exchange of attacks since the ceasefire took effect.

item.title: Coco Gauff's Wimbledon bid falls short, Djokovic facing Sinner next
item.description: Novak Djokovic is facing Jannik Sinner to attempt to reach his 11th Wimbledon final. This comes after Coco Gauff lost her latest bid in tennis. "60 Minutes" correspondent Jon Wertheim joins with more details.

item.title: I tried eating Erling Haaland's World Cup "Viking diet" for a day
item.description: Can a normal human handle soccer star Erling Haaland's calorific bonanza? CBS News' Leigh Kiniry found out the hard way.

item.title: Pentagon releases new set of UFO files: "Unlike anything I had seen"
item.description: The U.S. military released a new batch of files related to UFOs, including one report from a Navy pilot who said a mysterious object was "unlike anything I had seen" in 28 years of service.

item.title: Questions arise over new Air Force One after Trump uses old plane
item.description: The Secret Service advised that President Trump take the old plane to depart Turkey, according to people briefed on the situation.

item.title: Mexico seeks prosecutions over deaths in U.S. after fatal ICE shooting
item.description: Mexico's government has sharply criticized the treatment of its citizens under President Trump's push to increase deportations.

item.title: New video shows moments before ICE fatally killed Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston
item.description: New video appears to show the moments before U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston, Texas. CBS News' Jason Allen reports.

item.title: Iran buries former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei amid calls to kill Trump, tensions rising
item.description: Crowds held a long banner that said "Hey Trump we will kill you" as mourners gathered for the burial of former Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata reports, and contributor Joseph Zacks, a former deputy assistant director for counterterrorism for the CIA, joins with more insight.

item.title: Trump says he still won't sign housing bill, which will become law soon if he doesn't veto
item.description: President Trump didn't say Friday whether he will veto the legislation.

item.title: Can you switch debt relief companies after you've enrolled?
item.description: Thinking about changing debt relief companies? Here's what you need to know before making the move.

item.title: Hegseth frustrated with lack of adherence to grooming rules
item.description: Eight months ago, Hegseth told top military leaders there would be "no more beardos" and "fat troops."

item.title: Sean Evans says he wants Tom Cruise on "Hot Ones" as he prepares for new spinoff
item.description: "Hot Ones," hosted by Sean Evans, has become one of the hottest series on YouTube. He talks to Vladimir Duthiers about his celebrity-filled show, his new Netflix spinoff, "Hot Ones: Extra Heat," and who he wants to interview next.

item.title: Lifeguards demonstrate how to escape from a rip current
item.description: On sweltering summer days, many are beating the heat with a trip to the sea -- but ocean safety is key to a great beach day. Matt Gutman joined lifeguards to get a rip current safety demonstration.

item.title: Shoe factory fire in China kills dozens of workers
item.description: Flames engulfed a five-story shoe factory in Jinjiang in southern China, killing at least 28 people. The factory's owners and manager have been arrested. Anna Coren reports.

item.title: Graham Platner accuser reacts to dropped Senate bid, slams claims she spoke out over politics
item.description: Lyndsey Fifield, who accused Graham Platner of abuse during their relationship, spoke to The Free Press' Frannie Block after the Democrat dropped his bid for Senate in Maine. Platner has denied Fifield's claims. The Free Press and CBS News are owned by Paramount Skydance.

item.title: Author Meg Cabot revisits Genovia with "The Princess Diaries: The Graphic Novel"
item.description: Author Meg Cabot returns to Genovia to reinterpret "The Princess Diaries" in her new graphic novel, "The Princess Diaries: The Graphic Novel." Cabot tells "CBS Mornings" that she's excited to revisit the characters from the original films and "introduce them to a new generation."

item.title: Ukraine steps up drone attacks on Russia aided by American tech
item.description: Ukraine is using American technology to continue pounding Russia with drone attacks. CBS News Aidan Stretch reports.

item.title: Apache pilots' suspensions lifted after flyby over South Carolina beach
item.description: The decision to suspend the pilots quickly led to backlash online and drew the attention of Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

item.title: Breaking down Erling Haaland's 6,000-calorie daily Viking-like diet to stay World Cup ready
item.description: Norwegian soccer star Erling Haaland eats a massive 6,000-calorie daily diet that includes raw milk and bone marrow to stay fit. Leigh Kiniry took a stab at the World Cup breakout star's diet for a day.

item.title: How much interest will a $10,000 1-year CD account earn now?
item.description: Interest earnings on a CD account of this size and term will be substantial (and available in just 12 months).

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 12)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: Parents of teen found dead on island don't believe he chose to stay behind
item.description: Christine and Elmore Wonsley said they don't believe their son would've stayed on the island when his friends left by boat.

item.title: Nolan Wells' parents say they "don't believe he decided to stay on the island by himself"
item.description: The parents of Nolan Wells, the missing 18-year-old who was found dead on an island off Mississippi, said they don't believe their son volunteered to stay on the island by himself. Christine and Elmore Wonsley and their lawyer, Ben Crump, spoke about the case on "CBS Mornings."

item.title: Ex-Epoch Times CFO pleads guilty in $67 million money laundering scheme
item.description: The former CFO of The Epoch Times, a conservative multinational media company, interrupted jury selection at his money laundering trial to plead guilty to a conspiracy charge in a $67 million fraud scheme.

item.title: EasyJet accepts takeover offer from Apollo, over Castlerock's bid
item.description: British budget airline EasyJet says it's reached an agreement in principle for a £5.7-billion ($7.7-billion) takeover by U.S. private equity firm Apollo, topping a rival offer from fellow American private equity investor Castlelake​.

item.title: Pentagon releases 3rd batch of UFO files: "Are you seeing this?"
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released a new group of documents and videos related to UFOs, or UAPs, with 72 more documents, images and recordings.

item.title: Pentagon releases more UFO files: "Speechless after these observations"
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released a new batch of 64 files related to UFOs, unveiling a second tranche of records under an executive order by President Trump.

item.title: Pentagon begins releasing UFO files: "It's time the American people see"
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said in a statement that the documents "have long fueled justified speculation — and it's time the American people see it for themselves."
